# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_device_info (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `gather_subset` (String) When supplied, this argument will restrict the information returned to a given subset. You can specify a list of values to include a larger subset. Values can also be used with an initial `!` to specify that a specific subset should not be collected.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `data_increment` (Number) Specifies the paging increment at which device data is gathered from the API. Allows for greater control of the pace at which data is queried from the device. This setting is useful for setups with large configurations which may take a long time with the default values. While there is no limit to the value that can be specified, note that putting very large values with `gather_subset` set to meta choices like `all` might lead to module or device API crash. F5 recommends using `data_increment` custom values in tandem with `partition` and a specific `gather_subset` value for best experience.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `partition` (String) Specifies the partition to gather the resource information from. The default value for the partition is taken as Common.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `apm_access_policies` (String) Information about APM Access Policies.
- `apm_access_profiles` (String) Information about APM Access Profiles.
- `asm_policies` (String) Detailed information for ASM policies present on device.
- `asm_policy_stats` (String) Miscellaneous ASM policy related information.
- `asm_server_technologies` (String) Detailed information for ASM server technologies present on the device.
- `asm_signature_sets` (String) Detailed information for ASM signature sets present on device.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `client_ssl_profiles` (String) Client SSL Profile related information.
- `device_groups` (String) Device group related information.
- `devices` (String) Device related information.
- `external_monitors` (String) External monitor related information.
- `fasthttp_profiles` (String) FastHTTP profile related information.
- `fastl4_profiles` (String) FastL4 profile related information.
- `gateway_icmp_monitors` (String) Gateway ICMP monitor related information.
- `gtm_pools` (String) GTM pool related information. Every "type" of pool has the exact same list of possible information. Therefore, the list of information here is presented once instead of 6 times.
- `gtm_servers` (String) GTM server related information.
- `gtm_topology_regions` (String) GTM regions related information.
- `gtm_wide_ips` (String) GTM Wide IP related information. Every "type" of Wide IP has the exact same list of possible information. Therefore, the list of information here is presented once instead of 6 times.
- `http_monitors` (String) HTTP monitor related information.
- `http_profiles` (String) HTTP profile related information.
- `https_monitors` (String) HTTPS monitor related information.
- `iapp_services` (String) iApp v1 service related information.
- `icmp_monitors` (String) ICMP monitor related information.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `interfaces` (String) Interface related information.
- `irules` (String) iRule related information.
- `license` (String) License related info.
- `ltm_policies` (String) List of LTM (Local Traffic Manager) policies.
- `ltm_pools` (String) List of LTM (Local Traffic Manager) pools.
- `management_routes` (String) Management route related information.
- `nodes` (String) Node related information.
- `oneconnect_profiles` (String) OneConnect profile related information.
- `partitions` (String) Partition related information.
- `provision_info` (String) Module provisioning related information.
- `remote_syslog` (String) Remote Syslog related information.
- `route_domains` (String) Route domain related information.
- `self_ips` (String) Self IP related information.
- `server_ssl_profiles` (String) Server SSL related information.
- `software_hotfixes` (String) List of software hotfixes.
- `software_images` (String) List of software images.
- `software_volumes` (String) List of software volumes.
- `ssl_certs` (String) SSL certificate related information.
- `ssl_keys` (String) SSL certificate related information.
- `sync_status` (String) Configuration Synchronization Status across all Device Groups. Note that the sync-status works across all device groups - a specific device group cannot be queried for its sync-status. In general the device-group with the 'worst' sync-status will be shown.
- `system_db` (String) System DB related information.
- `system_info` (String) Traffic group related information.
- `tcp_half_open_monitors` (String) TCP Half-open monitor related information.
- `tcp_monitors` (String) TCP monitor related information.
- `tcp_profiles` (String) TCP profile related information.
- `traffic_groups` (String) Traffic group related information.
- `trunks` (String) Trunk related information.
- `ucs` (String) UCS backup related information
- `udp_profiles` (String) UDP profile related information.
- `users` (String) Details of the users on the system.
- `vcmp_guests` (String) vCMP related information.
- `virtual_addresses` (String) Virtual address related information.
- `virtual_servers` (String) Virtual address related information.
- `vlans` (String) List of VLAN information.
